title: Plaintext API keys in .secrets/ directory
description: .secrets/ contains plaintext API keys for Azure, GCP, OCI, and MiniMax. This directory likely commits to version control or is readable by unauthorized processes. Any compromise exposes all cloud credentials.
suggested_fix: Implement HashiCorp Vault or Azure Key Vault for secret storage. Use environment variables or a secrets manager library that decrypts at runtime. Add .secrets/ to .gitignore immediately.

title: No inter-service authentication between microservices
description: Services (geometry_onnx_interpreter, hse_encoder, execution_firewall, agent_service, request_validation, alert_manager, docker_helper) communicate without documented API authentication. MCP Server :8086 and LLM Gateway :8080 have no auth mentioned.
suggested_fix: Implement mTLS or JWT-based service mesh authentication. Add API keys or OAuth2 client credentials for all service-to-service communication. Configure network policies to restrict lateral movement.

title: Configuration drift across .qwen/.gemini/.serena
description: Three separate configuration directories (.qwen blueprints, .gemini AI settings, .serena LSP/memory) lack centralized versioning or consistency checks. Drift between these can cause runtime mismatches in behavior.
suggested_fix: Consolidate configs into a single location with schema validation. Implement config synchronization CI/CD step. Document all configuration options with expected values.

title: Missing test infrastructure and CI/CD pipeline
description: No test directories, test frameworks, or CI/CD configuration visible in the structure. Benchmark scripts exist but no unit/integration tests mentioned. This risks uncaught bugs in geometry kernel, JSON-RPC serialization, and service integration.
suggested_fix: Add pytest/unittest for each service. Create integration tests for service mesh. Set up GitHub Actions or similar for automated test runs before deployment.

title: MCP Server and LLM Gateway exposed without authentication
description: MCP Server on :8086 and LLM Gateway on :8080 are exposed but no authentication mechanism mentioned. These are critical entry points that could allow unauthorized access to AI services and downstream data.
suggested_fix: Add API key validation, OAuth2, or mTLS for both MCP Server and LLM Gateway. Restrict to internal network via firewall rules if possible. Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse.
